title: Optimal Control (LQR)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Optimal Control (LQR): LQR optimal control: find state feedback gain K minimizing infinite-horizon quadratic cost J = integral(x ** T Q x + u ** T R u) dt. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.riccati.algebraic…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; model_uncertainty_A_B, Q_R_mistuning set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: u = -K*x; K = R^{-1} B ** T P; P solves algebraic Riccati: A ** T P + P A - P B R^{-1} B ** T P + Q = 0
E.world_state_x: optimal control gain matrix K in R^{m x n}
E.observation_y: system matrices (A, B) and cost weights (Q, R) as inputs

W.regime: Existence of the recovered control_gain_matrix is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); model_uncertainty_A_B dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Deterministic sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: optimal cost J* = x0 ** T P x0; guaranteed stability margins: PM >= 60 deg, GM >= 6 dB. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |model_uncertainty_A_B|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 3) for Riccati equation solution per iteration
